problem_text_cn: 3-36 小球从水平地面上以初速$v_{0}$初抛出去，小球落地时在竖直方向上发生的非弹性碰撞恢复系数为$e$，小球与地面间的摩擦因数为$\mu$。若要求小球第一次与地面碰撞后能竖直弹起，试求小球可能达到的最大水平射程$s_{\text{max}}$。
problem_text_en: 3-36 A ball is projected from the horizontal ground with an initial speed $v_{0}$. The coefficient of restitution for the inelastic collision in the vertical direction when the ball hits the ground is $e$, and the coefficient of friction between the ball and the ground is $\mu$. The ball is required to bounce vertically after the first collision with the ground. Determine the maximum possible horizontal range $s_{\text{max}}$.
problem_solution_cn:
解 小球与地面第一次碰撞中，若地面摩擦力的作用可使小球水平分速度降为零，小球便能竖直弹起，显然小球水平速度可不太大，这对抛射角$\phi$是一种限制，在这一限制内寻求$s_{\text{max}}$。

小球竖直方向初速度、水平方向初速度及水平射程分别为
$$
v_{\perp}=v_{0}\sin\phi,\quad v_{y}=v_{0}\cos\phi,\quad s=\frac{v_{0}^{2}}{g}\sin 2\phi.
$$

落地后竖直方向反弹速度$v_{\perp}^{\prime}=ev_{\perp}$，对应的法向冲量为
$$
\bar{N}\Delta t_{N}=(1+e)mv_{0}\sin\phi,
$$
其中$m$为小球质量。小球受地面摩擦力提供的冲量大小为
$$
\bar{T}\Delta t_{f}\leqslant\mu\bar{N}\Delta t_{N}=\mu(1+e)mv_{0}\sin\phi,\quad\text{①}
$$

为使小球竖直弹起，要求
$$
\bar{T}\Delta t_{f}=mv_{y}=mv_{0}\cos\phi,\quad\text{②}
$$
①②式联立后，即要求
$$
\tan\phi\geqslant\frac{1}{\mu(1+e)}.
$$

下面分两种情况讨论。
(1) 若$\frac{1}{\mu(1+e)}\leqslant 1$，可取到$\phi\geqslant 45^{\circ}$，则得
$$
s_{\text{max}}=\frac{v_{0}^{2}}{g}\sin 2\phi\Big|_{\phi=45^{\circ}}=\frac{v_{0}^{2}}{g}.
$$

(2) 若$\frac{1}{\mu(1+e)}>1$，则可取的抛射角范围为
$$
\phi\geqslant\phi_{0}=\arctan\frac{1}{\mu(1+e)}>45^{\circ},
$$
此时$\pi>2\phi>\frac{\pi}{2}$。$\sin 2\phi$随$\phi$增大而减小，为使$\sin 2\phi$取最大，应取$2\phi$为最小，即取$\phi=\phi_{0}$，故有
$$
s_{\text{max}}=\frac{2v_{0}^{2}}{g}\sin\phi_{0}\cos\phi_{0}=\frac{2v_{0}^{2}\tan\phi_{0}}{g\sqrt{1+\tan^{2}\phi_{0}}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{1+\tan^{2}\phi_{0}}}.
$$
将$\tan\phi_{0}=\frac{1}{\mu(1+e)}$代入，即得
$$
s_{\text{max}}=\frac{2v_{0}^{2}}{g}\frac{\mu(1+e)}{\mu^{2}(1+e)^{2}+1}.
$$
problem_solution_en:
Solution In the first collision with the ground, if the frictional force can reduce the horizontal component of the ball’s velocity to zero, the ball can bounce vertically. Clearly, the horizontal velocity cannot be too large, which imposes a restriction on the projection angle $\phi$. Within this restriction, we seek $s_{\text{max}}$.

The vertical initial velocity, horizontal initial velocity, and horizontal range are respectively
$$
v_{\perp}=v_{0}\sin\phi,\quad v_{y}=v_{0}\cos\phi,\quad s=\frac{v_{0}^{2}}{g}\sin 2\phi.
$$
After landing, the vertical rebound speed is $v_{\perp}^{\prime}=ev_{\perp}$, and the corresponding normal impulse is
$$
\bar{N}\Delta t_{N}=(1+e)mv_{0}\sin\phi,
$$
where $m$ is the mass of the ball. The impulse provided by the frictional force is bounded by
$$
\bar{T}\Delta t_{f}\leqslant\mu\bar{N}\Delta t_{N}=\mu(1+e)mv_{0}\sin\phi,\quad\text{①}
$$
For a vertical bounce, we require
$$
\bar{T}\Delta t_{f}=mv_{y}=mv_{0}\cos\phi,\quad\text{②}
$$
Combining ① and ② gives the condition
$$
\tan\phi\geqslant\frac{1}{\mu(1+e)}.
$$
Now consider two cases.
(1) If $\frac{1}{\mu(1+e)}\leqslant 1$, we can take $\phi\geqslant 45^{\circ}$, and obtain
$$
s_{\text{max}}=\frac{v_{0}^{2}}{g}\sin 2\phi\Big|_{\phi=45^{\circ}}=\frac{v_{0}^{2}}{g}.
$$
(2) If $\frac{1}{\mu(1+e)}>1$, the admissible projection angles satisfy
$$
\phi\geqslant\phi_{0}=\arctan\frac{1}{\mu(1+e)}>45^{\circ},
$$
hence $\pi>2\phi>\frac{\pi}{2}$. Since $\sin 2\phi$ decreases as $\phi$ increases, to maximize $\sin 2\phi$ we should take the smallest $2\phi$, i.e. $\phi=\phi_{0}$. Therefore,
$$
s_{\text{max}}=\frac{2v_{0}^{2}}{g}\sin\phi_{0}\cos\phi_{0}=\frac{2v_{0}^{2}\tan\phi_{0}}{g\sqrt{1+\tan^{2}\phi_{0}}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{1+\tan^{2}\phi_{0}}}.
$$
Substituting $\tan\phi_{0}=\frac{1}{\mu(1+e)}$ yields
$$
s_{\text{max}}=\frac{2v_{0}^{2}}{g}\frac{\mu(1+e)}{\mu^{2}(1+e)^{2}+1}.
$$

The image background is white, the text is black, neatly arranged, containing the problem statement and a detailed solution process.
